title: Mental health and substance use disorders death rate by age group
subtitle: Estimated annual number of deaths by age group from mental health and substance use disorders per 100,000 people in the same age group. These include alcohol use, drug use, and eating disorders. Figures do not include deaths resulting from suicide, which are related to these disorders.
caption:
Mental health and substance use disorders death rate by age group

Estimated annual number of deaths by age group from mental health and substance use disorders per 100,000 people in the same age group. These include alcohol use, drug use, and eating disorders. Figures do not include deaths resulting from suicide, which are related to these disorders.

This horizontal bar chart displays the estimated annual number of deaths per 100,000 people attributed to mental health and substance use disorders across six distinct age groups for the year 2021. The data covers alcohol use, drug use, and eating disorders, while explicitly excluding deaths resulting from suicide. The age group of 50 to 69 years records the highest death rate on the chart, with a value of 9.2 per 100,000 people. The second-highest rate is observed in the 70 years and older category, which shows a value of 6.9. The "All ages" aggregate is recorded at 4.4, sitting just below the rate for the 15 to 49 years group, which stands at 4.5. The lower end of the age spectrum shows significantly lower figures; the 5 to 14 years group has a rate of less than 0.1, represented by a barely visible bar. The 0 to 4 years age group records a value of 0. The visual arrangement highlights a peak in mortality rates during middle age and older adulthood, with the 50-69 bracket exceeding the rates of both the younger adult population and the oldest demographic shown.
